title: World Cup Fans Are Smuggling Ranch Dressing Home. TSA Said No.
script: Nobody's smuggling ranch dressing — right? Wrong. International fans at the 2026 World Cup discovered ranch dressing this week, and they are not handling it calmly. Europeans flooded social media with stunned taste-test videos. Scottish fans drank Boston bars dry of beer AND ordered ranch. Then they tried to fly home with it — full bottles in carry-ons. The TSA had to step in. Their posts: Days since last airport ranch incident: 0. One World. One Ranch. Kraft launched TSA-compliant 3.4-ounce ranch packs so fans could bring it home. Swedish visitor Elsa Thora sparked the whole thing. A World Cup in America, and the main souvenir is Hidden Valley Ranch. So: is ranch dressing actually the best thing about America, or just the most surprising?
